item.title: A timeline of Nancy Guthrie's disappearance as search stretches on
item.description: Savannah Guthrie's mom, Nancy Guthrie, was reported missing Feb. 1.

item.title: Former Kevin McCarthy aide gives inside look at chaotic 2023 speaker race in new book
item.description: In January 2023, the House elected Kevin McCarthy as speaker after a historic and chaotic 15 roll-call votes. Former top McCarthy aide John Leganski looks back on that in his new memoir, "Glory, Grief, and the Gavel." Leganski joins "The Takeout" with more.

item.title: Clive Davis collaborator remembers music mogul as a "perfectionist" and "very giving"
item.description: After decades of building some of America's greatest hits, legendary music executive Clive Davis died Monday at 94. Narada Michael Walden, a singer-songwriter and record producer who worked closely with Davis, joins CBS News to reflect on the music mogul's legacy.

item.title: Trump plan would increase citizenship application fee by $570
item.description: A Trump administration plan would charge legal immigrants seeking citizenship $570 more in application fees while eliminating waivers and fee reductions for low-income applicants.

item.title: Bipartisan housing bill aims to boost homeownership. Could it work?
item.description: The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which has rare bipartisan support, would make it harder for major investors to hoard homes.

item.title: Senate passes landmark housing affordability bill after bipartisan breakthrough
item.description: The Senate passed a bill aimed at lowering housing costs on Monday after a major breakthrough and rare bipartisan consensus.

item.title: Gavin Newsom embracing Biden ahead of 2028, according to Axios
item.description: Many Democrats are trying to put as much distance as they can between themselves and former President Joe Biden. California Gov. Gavin Newsom, who is publicly weighing a presidential bid, is instead embracing Biden, according to Axios. Political strategists Joel Payne and T.W. Arrighi join with analysis.

item.title: Iowa governor candidate Rob Sand dismisses opponent's attack: "He's mostly been living in Kansas"
item.description: It's been more than 15 years since a Democrat occupied the governor's mansion in Iowa. State Auditor Rob Sand is looking to change that. Sand joins "The Takeout" to discuss his gubernatorial campaign and attacks from his Republican opponent, Zach Lahn.

item.title: New York congressional candidate Alex Bores lays out his plans to regulate AI
item.description: New York City Democrats will head to the polls on Tuesday to choose who they want to replace retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler. New York State Assembly Member Alex Bores, who is a part of the crowded primary field, joins "The Takeout" to discuss his priorities if elected to Congress.

item.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note, believed to be from abductor, said she died, sources say
item.description: Authorities believe two ransom notes addressed to Nancy Guthrie's family — including a note that said she had died — were likely sent by the person or group of people who abducted her.

item.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 2, leaves 6 survivors, in the Caribbean
item.description: The U.S. military has conducted another strike against a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the Caribbean, killing two and leaving six survivors, the U.S. Southern Command said.

item.title: LAUSD Superintendent Alberto Carvalho resigns amid FBI investigation
item.description: Nearly four months after federal agents raided his home and district headquarters, Superintendent Alberto Carvalho announced his resignation as the head of the Los Angeles Unified School District on Sunday.

item.title: Iran to let nuclear inspections resume as talks continue, Vance says
item.description: Vance says Iran agreed to let international nuclear inspectors back into the country during a "very, very good" first day of negotiations with the U.S.

item.title: 2 killed, including police officer, in shooting in Montreal
item.description: The suspect was shot and killed "right away," according to police, and there was no immediate word on a possible motive.

item.title: The Epstein factor in Keir Starmer's resignation as U.K. prime minister
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced Monday he's resigning as leader of the Labor Party and, in turn, as leader of the country. CBS News foreign correspondent Chris Livesay reports from London.

item.title: New poll captures Americans' concerns with Trump's handling of Iran war
item.description: A CBS News poll finds that most Americans want the war with Iran to end, and relatively few Americans believe the U.S. got the better of the new agreement. CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto breaks down the numbers.

item.title: Vance says talks with Iran set "good foundation" to end war
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Monday that Iran, after hours of negotiations, agreed to allow international nuclear inspectors inside the country. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe and Aaron MacLean have the latest.

item.title: How Alan Greenspan's "irrational exuberance" underlies today's politics
item.description: Alan Greenspan, who chaired the Federal Reserve under four U.S. presidents, died Monday at 100. In 1996, Greenspan warned about what he called "irrational exuberance." CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett explains how the phrase is exemplified in today's politics.

item.title: At least 2 killed in Montreal shooting, officials say
item.description: Authorities say at least two people, including a police officer, were killed during a shooting in a Montreal, Canada, neighborhood on Monday. Police say the suspect was also killed. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Alan Greenspan, Fed chair under 4 U.S. presidents, dies at age 100
item.description: Alan Greenspan's lengthy reign at the Federal Reserve coincided with a period of stability from the mid-1980s until 2007.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump administration's database of Americans' personal info
item.description: U.S. District Judge Sparkle Sooknanan said the administration violated the law when it created a centralized database of Americans' personal records.

item.title: Clive Davis, legendary music executive, dies at 94
item.description: Legendary music executive Clive Davis, who helped shape the careers of generations of artists including Janis Joplin, Bruce Springsteen and Whitney Houston, has died at 94. Jim Aswad, executive editor for Variety, joins CBS News to discuss Davis' life.

item.title: How much house can you afford with a $75,000 salary right now?
item.description: Thinking about buying a home? Here's what a $75,000 salary can realistically support in today's market.

item.title: What happens if you don't pay a deceased person's credit card debt?
item.description: A loved one's unpaid card balances don't always just disappear after death. Here's what their families should know.

item.title: Interstellar comet may be oldest object ever seen in solar system
item.description: Exactly where the comet 3I/ATLAS came from within the Milky Way remains a mystery.

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the man likely to be Britain's next prime minister?
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is resigning, and the man widely expected to replace him is a fellow Labour Party lawmaker known as the "King of the North."

item.title: Endangered pregnant whale found dead on bow of cruise ship in Alaska
item.description: A pregnant fin whale was found dead on the bow of a cruise ship in Alaska last week. Fin whales are endangered and particularly threatened by vessel strikes.

item.title: Is a debt collector contacting you while you're on disability? Here's what to do.
item.description: Your disability check is largely protected, but there are a few exceptions that could still leave it exposed.

item.title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
item.description: Keir Starmer will step down as U.K. Prime Minister weeks after a brutal round of local elections and as he faced a challenge from within his Labour Party.

item.title: The 2026 World Cup begins today. Here's what to know.
item.description: Charlie Davies, a former player for the U.S. Men's National Team, joins CBS News with more insight as the 2026 FIFA World Cup begins.
